rationale: Russian strikes killing nine in Kyiv are a continuation of the established war pattern with no qualitative escalation beyond prior baseline; the US-Iran deal remains the most impactful ongoing development for energy prices affecting Poland. Score adjusted down: ongoing situation without qualitative change.

top_events.title: Russian strikes kill nine and set historic cathedral in Kyiv ablaze
top_events.summary: Deadly Russian strikes on Kyiv continue the established war pattern near Poland's border. No qualitative escalation beyond the already-elevated baseline set last cycle.

top_events.title: US and Iran announce deal to end conflict; oil prices fall
top_events.summary: The US-Iran peace understanding is being confirmed across multiple sources, pushing oil prices down — a direct positive for Polish energy costs and inflation.

top_events.title: G7 meeting in France: agenda and attendees
top_events.summary: G7 discussions on trade, tariffs and geopolitics could indirectly affect EU economic policy relevant to Poland, but no confirmed outcomes yet.
